A user is inactive. Should access be allowed? Answer allow or deny.
The email is verified and the account is active. Should login be allowed? Answer allow or deny.
The email is unverified, but the account is active. Should login be allowed? Answer allow or deny.
Only admins may delete records. The user is an admin. Should deletion be allowed? Answer allow or deny.
Only admins may delete records. The user is not an admin. Should deletion be allowed? Answer allow or deny.
Access is allowed unless the account is suspended. The account is suspended. Should access be allowed? Answer allow or deny.
Access is allowed unless the account is suspended. The account is active and not suspended. Should access be allowed? Answer allow or deny.
If the user is an admin, allow access, except when the account is locked. The user is an admin and the account is locked. Answer allow or deny.
If a request is signed and the token is not expired, allow it. The request is signed but the token is expired. Answer allow or deny.
Login is allowed only if the email is verified. The account is active, but email status is not provided. Answer allow, deny, unknown, or depends.
Policy says allow if active and deny if flagged. Deny overrides allow. The user is active and flagged. Answer allow or deny.
Admin override permits access unless a legal hold exists. The user is an admin and a legal hold exists. Answer allow or deny.
Data export is allowed only if the manager approves and the user is active. The manager approved, but active status is unknown. Answer allow, deny, unknown, or depends.
Access is allowed if the user is not blocked and email is verified. Email is verified, but blocked status is unknown. Answer allow, deny, unknown, or depends.
Trial users can access reports only if billing is valid. Billing validity depends on the plan, and the plan is not specified. Answer allow, deny, unknown, or depends.
